scenarioStory.history: 22-year-old female found by her roommate surrounded by empty bottles of prescription benzodiazepines and an empty fifth of vodka. Roommate returned from work and found the patient unresponsive on the couch. Time of ingestion unknown but likely within the past 2-3 hours based on roommate leaving at 15:00.
scenarioStory.discussion: Differentiate sedative-hypnotic toxidrome from opioid toxidrome. Apply airway protection principles in overdose with intact hemodynamics but lost reflexes. Understand contraindications for flumazenil in mixed ingestion. Anticipate and mitigate aspiration risk during RSI. Choose appropriate induction and paralytic agents in a patient with on-board sedatives
scenarioStory.course: Arrival — Unresponsive with Soiled Airway: Crew finds an unresponsive young woman with critical airway compromise from emesis pooling and lost protective reflexes. Narcan has been given bystander without effect — consistent with non-opioid toxidrome. Respirations are slow and shallow, SpO2 is 82%, and the patient has no gag reflex. This is an airway protection case. → RSI for Airway Protection: Despite BVM and suction, patient has recurrent emesis and cannot protect her airway. GCS remains 6. Definitive airway is indicated for airway protection from aspiration, not because BVM cannot oxygenate. RSI is appropriate even with relatively stable hemodynamics. → Post-Intubation Supportive Care: Tube confirmed. Supportive care for sedative-hypnotic overdose: ventilation, gastric decompression, fluid resuscitation if needed, and prompt transport. Poison control contact recommended.

scenarioEvents.name: Arrival — Unresponsive with Soiled Airway
scenarioEvents.description: Crew finds an unresponsive young woman with critical airway compromise from emesis pooling and lost protective reflexes. Narcan has been given bystander without effect — consistent with non-opioid toxidrome. Respirations are slow and shallow, SpO2 is 82%, and the patient has no gag reflex. This is an airway protection case.

scenarioEvents.name: RSI for Airway Protection
scenarioEvents.description: Despite BVM and suction, patient has recurrent emesis and cannot protect her airway. GCS remains 6. Definitive airway is indicated for airway protection from aspiration, not because BVM cannot oxygenate. RSI is appropriate even with relatively stable hemodynamics.
relatedChecklist: Confirm tube placement with waveform capnography

scenarioEvents.name: Post-Intubation Supportive Care
scenarioEvents.description: Tube confirmed. Supportive care for sedative-hypnotic overdose: ventilation, gastric decompression, fluid resuscitation if needed, and prompt transport. Poison control contact recommended.

checklist.title: Recognize sedative-hypnotic toxidrome distinct from opioid overdose (naloxone won't work)
Place OPA and begin 2-person BVM with PEEP
Administer NS bolus pre-induction to mitigate hypotension risk
Perform RSI with Ketamine 1-2 mg/kg (100 mg) + Rocuronium 1 mg/kg (65 mg)
Confirm tube placement with waveform capnography
Minimal sedation post-intubation given on-board sedative load
Collect pill bottles and agents for the ED
12-lead EKG to screen for sodium channel effects
